items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. It preserves the mock-heroic phrasing (“vague doubts haunt me”) and the parallel structure that sets up the comedic ‘connection’ between two thefts across eras. No issues.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The repetitive, banal Soviet-style politeness is preserved and remains funny in contrast to the royal setting and Bunsha’s drunken impersonation. No issues.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Keeps the authoritarian, performative ‘royal’ demand that is central to the joke. No issues.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Maintains the elevated/archaic melancholy register that clashes comically with the Soviet domestic situation. No issues.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Correctly treats “демоны” as the agent and keeps the panicked, superstitious framing of a mundane technical situation. No issues.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Preserves the mock-biblical exclamation and the religious ‘miracle’ attribution that drives the joke (especially in Soviet atheist context). No issues.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Captures the emcee-like command that triggers mass dancing; works as a natural English equivalent for the performative cue. No issues.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Keeps the breezy, self-satisfied criminal cheerfulness and the ‘lucky drop-in’ vibe without over-explaining. No issues.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Crucially avoids calling the eggplant item “caviar” in English, preventing confusion while preserving the Soviet-status-symbol inversion joke. No issues.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Preserves the comic rhythm of the inventory and the suspiciously escalating/performative counting, including the trailing “Three.” No issues.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Keeps the calm, sing-song fatalism that turns the psychiatric threat into a shared absurdist punchline. No issues.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
